headlines.title: CHP investigates fatal crash on Hwy. 17 in Santa Cruz County
headlines.description: An investigation is underway after a fatal crash that prompted the closure of the right lane on southbound Highway 17 in Santa Cruz County on Saturday afternoon, the California Highway Patrol said. CHP said a vehicle crashed into a tree on southbound Highway 17, near Vine Hill Road, just after noon. Authorities reported the crash ended in fatality, and advised motorists the right lane of the highway would be closed for an unknown period of time while officers investigate. CHP has not released more information on this incident.

headlines.title: U.S. to play Round of 32 World Cup match in Santa Clara
headlines.description: It’s official! The United States is headed to the San Francisco Bay Area Stadium in Santa Clara. Paraguay’s victory over Türkiye on Friday secured the U.S. Men’s National Team’s place in the Round of 32. The result gave the Americans enough points to remain atop Group D and advance to the knockout stage. The USMNT will play its Round of 32 match at the San Francisco Bay Area Stadium on July 1 at 5 p.m. On secondary market platforms, tickets for the match start at $3,000; however, those who don’t have tickets and want to enjoy the excitement of the World Cup can go to the multiple Fan Zones throughout the Bay Area. For details, visit our 2026 FIFA World Cup guide here.

headlines.title: World Cup crowds have San Jose businesses bringing in more staff and alcohol
headlines.description: San Pedro Square in downtown San Jose has continued to see enormous crowds day after day for World Cup watch parties. Thursday, the area was crowded with more than 30,000 fans clamoring to watch the Mexico versus South Korea matchup, so many people that security had to turn some away. The San Jose Earthquakes have designated the square the official Soccer Celebration headquarters through July 19, and fans seem to have taken to the watch party environment in the blocked-off downtown street. These watch parties began on July 11, and now businesses in the area have had more than a week to adjust to the massive influx of traffic. “It’s been absolutely insane, the Super Bowl on steroids,” said Douglas Cookerly, VP of Operations for the Sushi Confidential bar and restaurant in San Pedro Square. In particular, businesses say that drinks have been in demand — especially given the warm weather. They noted that the most in-demand drink is beer, followed closely by tequila. Cookerly said he was expecting big crowds during the World Cup, but didn’t anticipate the popularity of the watch parties to rise to this intense level. He said much like the World Cup is the chance for teams to shine on a global stage, this period of time is a chance for San Jose businesses to shine and make an impression on people who’ve never been there before. “We’ve been ordering beer and liquor every single day, doubling up on the food, calling in the reserve staff, yeah, it’s been all hands on deck,” he added. Sushi Confidential has been so busy that owner Randy Musterer posted on social media that the business got an alert from San Jose water about a possible water leak, but it turns out the business was just using a lot of water to wash all those dishes and flush toilets. Friday, fans filled San Pedro Square throughout the day to watch three major matches: USA versus Australia, Brazil versus Haiti, and Turkey versus Paraguay. San Jose resident Luke Van Poetsch said he went to the square for the community watch party experience. “I mean, here you get the atmosphere, you get the feels, you hear the vuvuzelas going off, it really adds to the atmosphere,” he said. The energy isn’t just drawing in locals; plenty of international visitors are stopping by as well. “It’s really, really good vibes,” said Cai Clarke, who is the bay area, some people say its kind of boring, but I’m here and its absolute vibes, so I’m having a great time.

headlines.title: Alameda County Fair kicks off, attracting thousands to the fairgrounds
headlines.description: The Alameda County Fair kicked off on Friday, and thousands of people did not waste time filling their fairgrounds. With nearly half a million people expected over the next few weeks, organizers say keeping everyone safe is just as important as keeping them entertained. Alameda County Sheriff’s deputies will be patrolling the fairgrounds every day, and fair leaders say they are working hand-in-hand with law enforcement to keep things running smoothly. “Very close relationship with Alameda County Sheriffs meetings everyday briefing, they can have a safe fun time,” Alameda County Spokesperson Liz Rosinski said. The fair had options to eat everywhere; from favorites like funnel cakes and turkey legs, to new creations like spam wonton tacos, the fair continues to find new ways to keep people coming back. “We’re so excited we wait for this all year. The start of the county fair is really the start of summer,” Rosinski said. The fair is also a big boost for the local economy, generating more than $100 million each year. Pete Suratos has the full report in the video above.

headlines.title: Security increases in downtown San Jose as thousands expected for World Cup watch party
headlines.description: Tens of thousands of soccer fans are expected to gather in San Pedro Square for the Türkiye-Paraguay World Cup match watch party, and organizers said they are keeping safety a top priority. Organizers celebrated the big turnout in downtown San Jose after more than 30,000 fans came through for Thursday’s Mexico-South Korea matchup. Organizers expect as many as 25,000 people to pass through downtown for Friday’s Türkiye-Paraguay World Cup match watch party. With large crowds, city leaders say public safety is just as important as the celebration. “It’s focusing on the public safety and how do we create a fun family environment at the same time,” Executive Director of San Jose Sports Authority John Poch said. To help do that, organizers have significantly increased security throughout downtown San Jose. “There are over 50 police officers here, let alone the 40 to 50 private security that we have mainly for crowd control and traffic control,” Poch said. The goal: keeping the watch parties family-friendly while making sure fans can enjoy safely. “I feel like it’s pretty safe and we brought the kids and it feels fun so, happy to be here!” Aaron Scholl, a fan, said. And the excitement continues to draw visitors from around the world. “The atmosphere is just pretty amazing right now, so I’m just excited to be here and represent Australia,” said Tom Prak, a fan visiting from Melbourne, Australia. As attendance continues to grow, organizers are reminding fans to arrive early, expect delays and have backup plans in place. “It’s really patience. Come here for the atmosphere, come here for the cultural celebration, but come early and have a Plan B,” Poch said. Organizers said they’re continuing to monitor crowd sizes throughout the day and will adjust staffing as needed.

headlines.title: Türkiye fans flock to Santa Clara to watch team play in World Cup match
headlines.description: Thousands of Turkish Americans gathered in Santa Clara Friday to cheer on Türkiye in its World Cup match against Paraguay at San Francisco Bay Area Stadium. The Turkish American fans who showed up in the South Bay didn’t just come from around the Bay Area. They traveled from all over the world. “I met Turks from Australia, from Europe, from Asia, from Canada,” Ergun Kirlikovli of Los Angeles said. “Of course we’re like long lost brothers. We hug, we kiss, and we say where are you from and all that.” One of the prominent Turkish Americans on scene in Santa Clara was Hamit Altintop, a soccer hero in Türkiye. “All cultures, all communities, all generations are coming together and we are speaking only for one month just about fútbol,” he said. “This is insane.” The gathering near San Francisco Bay Area Stadium was just one of many events taking place Friday. The Ataturk cultural house in Mountain View, run by the Turkish American Association of California, will serve as another major gathering place throughout the tournament. “The World Cup means that we are one,” Turkish American Association President Nalan Ozisik said. “No matter what we think politically, how we observe our various religious beliefs or what our ethnicity is, none of that matters anymore. This is World Cup and Türkiye is there after 24 years. That’s all that matters today.” 2026 World Cup Jun 19 World Cup Jun 19 2026 World Cup Jun 18 Mexico becomes first country to reach knockout stage of World Cup, beating South Korea 1-0

headlines.title: Brush fire burns near Cal State East Bay campus
headlines.description: A brush fire was burning near the California State University, East Bay campus in Hayward Friday afternoon. The blaze broke out in the area of Harder Road and Westview Way. Additional information wasn’t immediately available.

headlines.title: Forward progress stopped in brush fire near San Jose International Airport
headlines.description: A vegetation fire that burned Friday afternoon just south of the San Jose International Airport has been contained, firefighters said. The blaze was reported in the area of Coleman Avenue and Anita Street. Fire officials said the blaze was burning with a medium rate of spread before crews were able to stop forward progress. Traffic was impacted in the area while firefighters battled the blaze. This story uses functionality that may not work in our app. Click here to open the story in your web browser.

headlines.title: Thousands gather to celebrate Juneteenth in the East Bay
headlines.description: In the East Bay, thousands of people gathered to celebrate Juneteenth, the day the nation’s last remaining enslaved people learned they were free. “Juneteenth celebration is a celebration of the freedom of slaves, so as a result we want to celebrate African Americans,” Juneteenth organizer Mika McCants said. And African American pride was on full display. Bay Street Emeryville is hosting its third annual Bay Area Black Expo; more than 100 black owned businesses and artisans set up shop to showcase the rich culture of African Americans. “It’s important for our young people to really understand where they come from so having days like this and having them come out and see the beautifulness, the richness of our culture. There are different hues of Black people, we come from different avenues and being able to come together culturally is important,” Dr. Dela Morris said. The expo also included a fashion show featuring the unique creations of local black designers including Cardum Sage of Oakland, who just returned from New York Fashion Week. “I grew up here and I got my artistry really at home and this is the place. This is the hot bed of innovation,” Sage said. In Contra Costa County, the city of Antioch’s Juneteenth event is focused on families. Kids are enjoying carnival rides, and the city has an evening of family friendly performances planned. Those out celebrating say it’s a time to recognize their community’s resilience and talent. “Got to make time to look at African-Americans and see what they’ve done to survive. Comedy laughter, going out dressing up the best you can, having some fun,” Kalid Maky said. “We are designers were architects. We are philanthropists. There’s so much Rich history that people need to know about African-Americans,” Morris said.

headlines.title: San Francisco Embarcadero Plaza waterfront park design approved
headlines.description: The San Francisco Recreation and Park Commission unanimously voted Thursday to approve the final design for the $40 million Embarcadero Plaza waterfront park. As part of the city’s economic recovery and downtown revitalization strategy, the 5-acre space at the foot of Market Street will transform into a large central lawn, playground, community gathering space and outdoor dining area, a city news release said. “This new and ambitious waterfront park is part of our vision for a downtown where people live, work, play, and learn and will help us build on our momentum downtown — because when downtown succeeds, our entire city succeeds,” said Mayor Daniel Lurie in the release. The $40 million project is being supported by both in the private and public sector, with $20 million being secured by philanthropic nonprofit Crankstart and the San Francisco Downtown Development Corporation, and $18 million secured from public funding sources. According to the news release, the San Francisco Recreation and Park Department will oversee the project breaking ground in late 2026 and will support long-term upkeep. On Tuesday, Supervisor Danny Sauter, whose District 3 includes Embarcadero Plaza, introduced legislation for the city to accept up to $20 million in private funding for the waterfront park project. In the news release, he said the renovation will be the “gateway from San Francisco’s downtown to our Bay,” and will bring more art, culture and recreation opportunities. The release said the large space will host festivals, concerts, picnic areas and gardens to connect the waterfront to downtown. The final concept design approved Thursday reflects more than a yearlong effort of community engagement through public meetings, survey responses and comments from residents and workers. The city release said the project also plans to integrate “historic interpretation elements” to show the Embarcadero’s evolution throughout history, including the original plaza with the controversial Vaillancourt Fountain and the Embarcadero Freeway, a 1.2-mile, double-decker elevated roadway that operated from 1959 to 1991. “Great cities are defined by their public spaces,” said Shola Olatoye, CEO of the San Francisco Downtown Development Corporation. “The Embarcadero was once shaped by a freeway. Now it will be shaped by the people who use it, 5 acres connecting downtown to the Bay with room for concerts, markets, and kids having fun on the playground.”

headlines.title: California sees gas prices fall for nearly a month straight
headlines.description: The average price of a gallon of regular gasoline in California fell 16 cents to $5.64 in the past week, marking nearly four consecutive weeks of price decreases at the pump, AAA announced Thursday. “Crude oil prices are down as the U.S. and Iran reach a deal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz,” AAA Mountain West Group spokesperson Doug Johnson said in a statement. “These sliding gas prices are welcome news for millions of people who are preparing to travel for Independence Day in record numbers starting next weekend.” Here’s a look at the average price of gas in cities across Central and Northern California, per AAA: San Francisco: $5.77 Oakland: $5.70 San Jose: $5.65 Sacramento: $5.65 Fresno: $5.58 Stockton: $5.51 On the national scale, the average price for a gallon of regular gasoline is down to $3.99 for the first time since March 30, according to AAA. GasBuddy said fuel prices won’t move fast until peace in the Middle East is a sure thing. “In the interim, if oil prices abruptly go right back up, that’s going to pull the rug under gas stations’ feet and they could raise prices very quickly again,” GasBuddy’s Patrick De Haan said. War with Iran Jun 18 Oil & Gas Jun 14 Oil and gas prices could take months to return to normal after Iran deal

headlines.title: List: Bay Area watch parties for U.S. vs. Australia World Cup match
headlines.description: For Friday’s United States versus Australia match of the FIFA World Cup 2026 starting at noon, fans will get to choose from several watch parties around the Bay Area to see the game live. In the South Bay, San Pedro Square Market in San Jose and surrounding streets have been turned into an interactive fan zone with several large screens to watch the match. Hosted by the San Jose Earthquakes, attendees can choose from numerous restaurants in San Pedro Square with World Cup-themed menus and special drinks. It will feature entertainment zones where fans can consume alcoholic beverages in designated outdoor areas while they watch the game. Merchandise will also be available as well as a family-friendly area with face-painting and interactive games. Admission is free with an RSVP. There will also be watch parties at more than 15 restaurants at Santana Row shopping area in San Jose, called the “Row Cup.” A FIFA pop-up shop, live music, and a happy hour. Here’s a full list of participating restaurants. State Street Market, an indoor food hall in Los Altos, will also be playing the match on several large screens. There will also be numerous viewing opportunities in San Francisco. SPARK Social SF in the Mission Bay Area will have food trucks, large screens, and a beer garden for the match. Fans can also enjoy the game on an outdoor screen at Pier 39, accompanied by the pier’s iconic sea lions. Attendees will have the opportunity to snap photos with a giant FIFA World Cup soccer ball statue. Several Pier 39 restaurants will also be streaming the game, and some shops, including Lids and Bubba Gump Shrimp Co., will have exclusive FIFA merchandise. USMNT Jun 17 May 13 There will also be free screenings at China Basin Park, Salesforce Park, the Crossing at East Cut, and the Gold Bar Distillery on Treasure Island. In the North Bay, a watch party will be hosted at the Ruins in American Canyon. The Ruins is an open-air venue set in the ruins of an abandoned cement factory with industrial architecture and graffiti art. The family-friendly watch party will feature food, drinks, and several large screens. Admission is free with RSVP. Fans in the East Bay will also have several options to watch the game. Donkey and Goat Winery in Berkeley will play the match with live music, curated wines, collector’s wine glasses, outdoor games and activities. The first 50 attendees will get to print silk-screen game day t-shirts for free. Another watch party will take place at the Oakland Marriott City Center. Local vendors will sell food and drink, and complementary light snacks will be available for attendees. The Alameda County Fair will also host the match with crafted cocktails and a beer bar. The viewing is free with a ticket to the county fair.
